flagged.fraud: <0>This account has been flagged as FRAUD. Sending is not allowed.</0>

non-active.title: Non-Activated Account
non-active.line1: You are attempting to send funds to a non-activated account.
non-active.line2: This account has never been used and currently has a zero balance.
non-active.reserve: If you continue, {{amount}} will be used to activate the account on the ledger.

remit.hint: - Send any token and pay for destination reserves.
remit.disabled-account: This destination account has incoming remit disabled.
remit.cannot-use: You cannot use Remit to send tokens to this account.
remit.choose-other: Please uncheck the "Use Remit" option or choose a different destination.
remit.title: Remit Transaction
remit.description: When using Remit, you can send any token to the destination account, even if they don't have a trustline for it.
remit.note-text: You will pay for the destination account's reserve requirements if the account needs to be activated.
remit.token-selection-text: All available tokens (including native XAH) are shown since remit allows sending any token regardless of trustlines.
remit.xahau-only: This feature is only available on the Xahau network.
remit.error-disabled: Cannot use Remit: Destination account has incoming remit disabled.

send.mint-note: To mint a token (by sending a payment to a distribution account that already has a trustline to you), you must be logged in with the issuer address.

escrow.title: Create Escrow
escrow.unlock-after: Unlock after <0>(when funds can be released)</0>
escrow.condition-warning: If you lose it, you won't be able to release the funds.

errors.advanced-pro: Advanced options (fee, source tag, condition) are available only to logged-in Bithomp Pro subscribers.
errors.unlock-or-condition: You must specify either an unlock time or a condition (or both).
errors.conditional-time: A conditional escrow must have either an unlock time or an expiration time (or both).
errors.condition-hex: Condition must be a valid hexadecimal string.
errors.condition-characters: Condition must contain only hexadecimal characters.
errors.unlock-future: Unlock time must be in the future.
errors.cancel-future: Cancel time must be in the future.
errors.cancel-after-unlock: Cancel time must be after unlock time.

escrow.intro: On this page, you can create an escrow to securely lock {{nativeCurrency}} until time-based or condition-based requirements are met.
escrow.fulfillment-warning: We do not save/keep the Fulfillment. Please copy and save it securely.

trustline.title: Set Trustline
trustline.description: Trustset: Set a Trustline to an address
trustline.heading: Set/Update Trust (Trustlines)
trustline.intro: Create or modify a Trustline linking two accounts.
trustline.learn-text: Trustlines are structures in the {{explorerName}} for holding tokens. Trustlines enforce the rule that you cannot cause someone else to hold a token they don't want. <0>Learn more about {{explorerName}} Trustlines</0>.
trustline.limit-total-supply: the Limit will be set to the total supply:
trustline.token-info-warning: We do not take responsibility for the accuracy of the token descriptions or related information. Users should always do their own research (DYOR). The content is for informational purposes only, not financial advice.
trustline.currency-placeholder: Currency code (e.g., USD, myCurrency or HEX)
trustline.limit: Limit (The maximum amount you want to trust)
trustline.limit-placeholder: Enter the maximum amount you want to trust
trustline.rippling-text: Controls indirect movement of your funds. (Disallow unless you are a token issuer)
trustline.freeze-text: Freezes the counterparty's ability to send the frozen currencies to others, but the counterparty can still receive those currencies.
trustline.deep-freeze-text: The counterparty cannot receive funds until or unless their Trustline is unfrozen.
trustline.authorize-text: Grants the counterparty permission to hold currency issued by this account.
trustline.authorize-warning: Once enabled, it cannot be revoked.
trustline.quality-text: the exchange rate for this trustline.
trustline.quality-in-text: % of incoming funds kept by the sender.
trustline.quality-out-text: % of outgoing funds kept by the issuer.
trustline.quality-example-text: If QualityIn or QualityOut is set to 1%, then for every 100 units sent, 1 unit is kept as a quality adjustment.
trustline.quality-separate: These are separate from token transfer fees.

errors.valid-limit: Please enter a valid limit (must be 0 or positive).
errors.quality-in: QualityIn must be a valid positive number.
errors.quality-out: QualityOut must be a valid positive number.

errors.source-tag: Please enter a valid source tag.
errors.invoice-id: Please enter a valid invoice ID.

check.intro: A check lets you send funds that the recipient can claim later.

account-delete.title: Account delete
account-delete.description: Delete your account by sending all funds to another account.
account-delete.alreadyDeleted: The account {{address}} has already been deleted on the {{explorerName}} Ledger.
account-delete.intro: An Account delete transaction permanently deletes an account and any objects it owns on the {{explorerName}} Ledger, if possible, and transfers the account’s remaining {{nativeCurrency}} to a specified destination account.
account-delete.feeInfo: The fee to execute this transaction is {{fee}}. This is a protocol requirement of the {{explorerName}} Ledger, and {{fee}} is permanently burned as part of the process.
account-delete.warning: Attention: Do NOT use an exchange or custodial wallet as the destination address. You may permanently lose your funds.
account-delete.spamWarning: This account has been flagged for spam. Proceed with caution.
account-delete.potentialFraudWarning: This account has been flagged as potentially involved in fraud, scams, or phishing. Proceed with caution.
account-delete.fraudWarning: This account has been flagged as FRAUD. Sending is not allowed.
account-delete.nonActiveWarning: You can not send funds to a non-activated account.
account-delete.advancedPro: available to logged-in Bithomp Pro subscribers
account-delete.destinationTagWarning: I acknowledge that the destination account MUST NOT be an exchange or custodial wallet, as this may lead to a loss of funds.
account-delete.agreeTerms: I agree with the <0>Terms and conditions</0>.
account-delete.agreeFlagged: I understand the risks and I want to proceed with sending funds to this flagged account

amm.deposit-title: Deposit assets into an AMM Pool
amm.deposit-description: Add liquidity to an Automated Market Maker Pool
amm.withdraw-title: Withdraw assets from an AMM Pool
amm.withdraw-description: Get back your assets from an Automated Market Maker Pool
amm.withdraw-heading: Withdraw assets from an AMM Pool
amm.vote-title: Vote for a fee on an AMM Pool
amm.vote-description: Vote for a fee on an Automated Market Maker Pool
amm.vote-heading: Vote for a fee for an AMM Pool

fields.effectivePriceAsset: Effective Price per Unit of Asset

create.risks: I acknowledge and understand the risks associated with XRPL AMMs, including impermanent loss, fund withdrawal risks, market volatility, and the impact of creating unbalanced pools. I agree to participate at my own risk.

deposit.risks: I acknowledge and understand the risks associated with XRPL AMMs, including impermanent loss, fund withdrawal risks, market volatility, and the impact of depositing into unbalanced pools. I agree to participate at my own risk.

withdraw.risks: I acknowledge and understand the risks associated with XRPL AMMs, including impermanent loss, fund withdrawal risks, market volatility, and the impact of withdrawing from unbalanced pools. I agree to participate at my own risk.
withdraw.withdrawAllInfo: This will withdraw all of your LP Tokens and return as much as possible of both assets.

vote.intro: Vote on the trading fee for an Automated Market Maker instance. Up to 8 accounts can vote in proportion to the amount of the AMM's LP Tokens they hold. Each new vote re-calculates the AMM's trading fee based on a weighted average of the votes.

tfSingleAsset.label: Single Asset Deposit
tfSingleAsset.description: Deposit exactly the specified amount of one asset, and receive an amount of LP Tokens based on the resulting share of the pool (minus fees).

tfTwoAsset.label: Double Asset Deposit
tfTwoAsset.description: Deposit both of this AMM's assets, up to the specified amounts. The actual amounts deposited must maintain the same balance of assets as the AMM already holds, so the amount of either one deposited MAY be less than specified. The amount of LP Tokens you get in return is based on the total value deposited.

tfTwoAssetIfEmpty.label: Empty AMM Deposit
tfTwoAssetIfEmpty.description: Deposit both of this AMM's assets, in exactly the specified amounts, to an AMM with an empty asset pool. The amount of LP Tokens you get in return is based on the total value deposited.

tfLPToken.label: LP Token Target
tfLPToken.description: Deposit both of this AMM's assets, in amounts calculated so that you receive the specified amount of LP Tokens in return. The amounts deposited maintain the relative proportions of the two assets the AMM already holds. The amount of LP Tokens you get in return is based on the total value deposited.

tfOneAssetLPToken.label: Single Asset + LP Target
tfOneAssetLPToken.description: Deposit up to the specified amount of one asset, so that you receive exactly the specified amount of LP Tokens in return (after fees).

tfLimitLPToken.label: Single Asset + Price Limit
tfLimitLPToken.description: Deposit up to the specified amount of one asset, but pay no more than the specified effective price per LP Token (after fees).

tfLPToken.label: LP Token Withdrawal
tfLPToken.description: Return the specified amount of LP Tokens and receive both assets from the AMM's pool in amounts based on the returned LP Tokens' share of the total LP Tokens issued.

tfWithdrawAll.label: Withdraw All
tfWithdrawAll.description: Return all of your LP Tokens and receive as much as you can of both assets in the AMM's pool.

tfTwoAsset.label: Double Asset Withdrawal
tfTwoAsset.description: Withdraw both of this AMM's assets, in up to the specified amounts. The actual amounts received maintains the balance of assets in the AMM's pool.

tfSingleAsset.label: Single Asset Withdrawal
tfSingleAsset.description: Withdraw exactly the specified amount of one asset, by returning as many LP Tokens as necessary.

tfOneAssetWithdrawAll.label: Single Asset Withdraw All
tfOneAssetWithdrawAll.description: Withdraw at least the specified amount of one asset, by returning all of your LP Tokens. Fails if you can't receive at least the specified amount.

tfOneAssetLPToken.label: Single Asset + LP Token
tfOneAssetLPToken.description: Withdraw up to the specified amount of one asset, by returning up to the specified amount of LP Tokens.

tfLimitLPToken.label: Single Asset + Price Limit
tfLimitLPToken.description: Withdraw up to the specified amount of one asset, but pay no more than the specified effective price in LP Tokens per unit of the asset received.

nft-mint.heading: Mint a new NFT
nft-mint.intro: You can use this page to create a new NFT, a unique digital asset that can be used in a variety of applications.
nft-mint.uriLabel: URI that points to the data or metadata associated with the NFT:
nft-mint.taxonLabel: NFT Taxon (collection identifier, leave as 0 for the issuer's first collection):

nft-mint.burnable: Burnable (can be destroyed by the issuer)

nft-mint.mintForOther: Mint on behalf of another account
nft-mint.authorizedMinterNote: Note: You must be authorized as a minter for this account, or the transaction will fail.
nft-mint.digestHelp: The digest is calculated from the metadata. It is used to verify that the URI and the metadata have not been tampered with.
nft-mint.mintAndSendReserve: You will pay the Object Reserve in XAH for the NFT to be held on the Destination account.

account-settings.title: Account settings
account-settings.signedIn: Manage your account settings on the {{explorerName}}.
account-settings.signedOut: Sign in to your account to manage your account settings.
account-settings.hexOnly: Must contain only hexadecimal characters (0-9, a-f, A-F)
account-settings.evenHex: Must have an even number of characters (pairs of hex digits)
account-settings.minChars: Must be at least {{count}} characters ({{bytes}} bytes)
account-settings.firstByte: First byte must be 02 or 03 for secp256k1 keys, or ED for Ed25519 keys. Current: 0x{{byte}}
account-settings.tickRange: Must be between 3 and 15 (or 0 to clear)
account-settings.domainHelp: Enter your domain. It will be stored on-ledger.
account-settings.emailHelp: Enter an email or a 32-character hex MD5. Leave empty and press Clear to remove.
account-settings.messageKeyHelp: Provide a hex-encoded public key (exactly 66 characters/33 bytes). First byte must be 0x02 or 0x03 for secp256k1 keys, or 0xED for Ed25519 keys. Used for encrypted messaging.
account-settings.tickHelp: Controls significant digits for order book prices. 0 clears.
account-settings.walletHelp: Optional 64-character hexadecimal hash locator for your wallet application.
account-settings.nftHelp: Enter the address that will be authorized to mint NFTokens for this account
account-settings.nftChangeHelp: To change the authorized minter, first clear the current one, then set a new one.
account-settings.errNft: Please enter a valid NFTokenMinter address.
account-settings.errEmailEmpty: Please enter an email or a 32-character hex MD5 hash.
account-settings.errEmail: Enter a valid email or a 32-character hex MD5 hash.
account-settings.errMessageKeyEmpty: MessageKey cannot be empty. Please enter a hex-encoded public key.
account-settings.errTick: TickSize must be 0 to clear or an integer between 3 and 15.
account-settings.errWalletEmpty: WalletLocator cannot be empty. Please enter a 64-character hexadecimal string.
account-settings.errSignIn: Please sign in to your account.
account-settings.errPro: Advanced options are available only to logged-in Bithomp Pro subscribers.
account-settings.errOwnerDir: Can only be enabled if account has no trustlines, offers, escrows, payment channels, checks, or signer lists
account-settings.errGlobalFreeze: Cannot change Global Freeze when No Freeze is enabled

requireDestTag.name: Destination Tag
requireDestTag.description: If enabled, incoming transactions must include a Destination Tag.

disallowXRP.name: {{nativeCurrency}} receiving
disallowXRP.description:
If enabled, this means the account does not accept {{nativeCurrency}} payments.
Note: This setting is not enforced by the protocol, so {{nativeCurrency}} can still be sent to the account.

disallowIncomingCheck.name: Incoming Checks
disallowIncomingCheck.description: If enabled, other accounts cannot create Checks with this account as the destination.

disallowIncomingPayChan.name: Incoming Payment Channels
disallowIncomingPayChan.description: If enabled, other accounts cannot create Payment Channels with this account as the destination.

disallowIncomingTrustline.name: Incoming Trustlines
disallowIncomingTrustline.description: If enabled, other accounts cannot create trustlines to this account.

depositAuth.name: Deposit Authorization
depositAuth.description: If enabled, this account can only receive funds from accounts it has pre-authorized.

disallowIncomingNFTokenOffer.name: Incoming NFT Offers
disallowIncomingNFTokenOffer.description: If enabled, other accounts cannot create NFT offers with this account as the destination.

disallowIncomingRemit.name: Incoming Remit
disallowIncomingRemit.description: If enabled, other accounts cannot send Remit transactions to this account.

tshCollect.name: Transactional Stakeholder (TSH) Collect
tshCollect.description: If enabled, this account can collect TSH rewards from transactions.

requireAuth.name: Authorization
requireAuth.description: If enabled, trustlines to this account require authorization before they can hold tokens. Can only be enabled if the account has no trustlines, offers, escrows, payment channels, checks, or signer lists.

allowTrustLineClawback.name: Trustline Clawback
allowTrustLineClawback.description: Allow account to claw back tokens it has issued. Can only be set if the account has an empty owner directory (no trustlines, offers, escrows, payment channels, checks, or signer lists). After you set this flag, it cannot be reverted. The account permanently gains the ability to claw back issued assets on trustlines.

allowTrustLineLocking.name: Trustline Locking
allowTrustLineLocking.description: Allow Trustline tokens issued by this account to be held in escrow. If not enabled, tokens issued by this account can't be escrowed. After you enable this flag, it cannot be disabled.

defaultRipple.name: Default rippling
defaultRipple.description: This is a setting that token issuers need to enable. If enabled, allows rippling on all trustlines by default. This affects how payments flow through your account.

disableMaster.name: Master Key
disableMaster.description:
Disabling the master key pair removes one method of authorizing transactions. You should be sure you can use one of the other ways of authorizing transactions, such as with a regular key or by multi-signing, before you disable the master key pair. (For example, if you assigned a regular key pair, make sure that you can successfully submit transactions with that regular key.) Due to the decentralized nature of the {{explorerName}}, no one can restore access to your account if you cannot use the remaining ways of authorizing transactions.
You should do this if your account's master key pair may have been compromised, or if you want to make multi-signing the only way to submit transactions from your account.
To disable the master key pair, you must use the master key pair. However, you can re-enable the master key pair using any other method of authorizing transactions.

globalFreeze.name: Global Freeze
globalFreeze.description: If enabled, freezes all tokens issued by this account, preventing them from being transferred. This affects all trustlines for tokens you have issued. Cannot be enabled if No Freeze is active. Use with caution as it impacts all token holders.

noFreeze.name: No Freeze
noFreeze.description: If enabled, permanently gives up the ability to freeze tokens issued by this account. This setting cannot be reversed.

account-control.title: Account Management
account-control.description: Manage regular key, master key, and account access on the ledger.
account-control.introSignedIn: Manage signing authority for your account on {{explorerName}}.
account-control.introSignedOut: Sign in to your account to manage account control settings.
account-control.proLoginNotice: Account Control is available to logged-in Bithomp Pro subscribers.
account-control.proExpired: Your Bithomp Pro subscription has expired.
account-control.regularKeyDescription: A Regular Key is an alternative address whose private key can sign transactions for your account. Unlike the master key, you can replace or remove it at any time. Use it to keep your master key in cold storage while signing day-to-day transactions with a hot wallet.
account-control.regularKeyNote: Bithomp currently does not support signing transactions with a Regular Key.
account-control.regularKeyPlaceholder: Enter address to use as Regular Key
account-control.regularKeyHelp: Enter the address whose private key you want to use to sign transactions.
account-control.multiSigDescription: Multi-signing lets a group of accounts collectively authorize transactions. Each signer has a weight; a transaction is valid when the combined weight of signers reaches the quorum. Useful for shared treasury accounts or extra security.
account-control.multiSigNote: Bithomp currently does not support signing transactions with Multi-signature.
account-control.quorumHelp: Minimum total weight required to sign a transaction.
account-control.masterKeyDisabledText: The master key is disabled. You must sign with the regular key or multi-sig. To re-enable it, sign this transaction with the regular key or a signer — not the master key.
account-control.masterKeyEnabledText: Disabling the master key means the original account private key can no longer sign transactions. Ensure you have a working Regular Key or Signer List first. Due to the decentralized nature of {{explorerName}}, no one can restore access if you lose all other signing methods.
account-control.masterKeyNote: Bithomp does not yet support signing with a Regular Key or a Signer List.
account-control.masterKeyMustSet: You must set a Regular Key or a Signer List before disabling the Master Key.
account-control.blackholeWarning: This is irreversible. Blackholing permanently removes the ability for anyone — including you — to sign transactions from this account. The account can still receive funds, but they can never be moved out. Used to create provably unspendable accounts (e.g. to lock tokens forever or prove an issuer has surrendered control).
account-control.blackholeStep1Help: Set the Regular Key to {{address}} — the all-zeros address for which no private key exists.
account-control.blackholeConfirm: I understand blackholing is permanent and irreversible. No one will ever be able to sign transactions from this account again, and any funds sent here will be locked forever with no way for anyone to access or recover them.
account-control.blackholeStep1: Set Regular Key to blackhole address

errors.regularSame: Regular key cannot be the same as the account address.
errors.removeRegularUnsafe: Cannot remove the regular key when the master key is disabled and there is no signer list — you would lose access to your account.
errors.signerInvalid: Signer "{{account}}" is not a valid address or username.
errors.signerSelf: A signer cannot be the account itself.
errors.signerWeight: Each signer weight must be a whole number between 1 and 99.
errors.quorum: Quorum must be a whole number ≥ 1.
errors.quorumTooHigh: Quorum ({{quorum}}) cannot exceed total signer weight ({{totalWeight}}).
errors.removeSignerUnsafe: Cannot remove the signer list when the master key is disabled and there is no regular key — you would lose access to your account.
errors.disableMasterUnsafe: You must set a Regular Key or a Signer List before disabling the Master Key, otherwise you will permanently lose access to your account.
errors.regularUnsupported: Regular Key signing is not supported yet.
errors.signerUnsupported: Signer List signing is not supported yet.
errors.bothUnsupported: Regular Key and Signer List signing are not supported yet.
errors.addSignerFirst: Add at least one signer first.
errors.setRegularOrSigner: Set a Regular Key or Signer List first.
errors.regularAlreadyBlackhole: Regular Key is already set to the blackhole address.
errors.signInTooltip: Sign in to your account to enable this function.
errors.proLoginTooltip: Log in to Bithomp Pro to enable this function.
errors.proSubscribeTooltip: Subscribe to Bithomp Pro to enable this function.

success.blackholeKeySet: Blackhole regular key set. Proceed to Step 2 to disable the master key.
success.blackholed: Account has been blackholed. No one, including you, can ever sign transactions for it again.

token-issuer-settings-page.title: Token Issuer Settings
token-issuer-settings-page.description: Manage required authorization, rippling, freeze and other token issuer settings.
token-issuer-settings-page.introSignedIn: Configure your token issuance settings on the {{explorerName}}.
token-issuer-settings-page.introSignedOut: Sign in to your account to manage Token Issuer Settings.
token-issuer-settings-page.proNotice: Token Issuer Settings are available to logged-in Bithomp Pro subscribers.
token-issuer-settings-page.availablePro: Available only to logged-in Bithomp Pro subscribers.
token-issuer-settings-page.transferRateHelp: Percentage fee issuer charges on transfers of issued tokens.
token-issuer-settings-page.domainHelp: Enter your domain. It will be stored on-ledger.

errors.signIn: Please sign in to your account.
errors.proOnly: Token Issuer Settings are available only to logged-in Bithomp Pro subscribers.

issue-mpt.title: Issue an MPT
issue-mpt.seoDescription: Create and sign a Multi-Purpose Token issuance on the XRP Ledger.
issue-mpt.intro: Define the supply, precision, metadata, and permanent permissions of a new Multi-Purpose Token.

hints.scale: From 0 to 255. This cannot be changed after issuance.
hints.maximum: Optional. If omitted, the protocol default applies.
hints.baseUnits: Stored on ledger as {{amount}} base units.
hints.transferFee: Optional, from 0 to 50 with up to 3 decimals.

permissions.title: Token permissions
permissions.warning: Choose carefully: these capabilities cannot be enabled later.

canLock.title: Can lock
canLock.description: Issuer can lock individual or all balances.

canEscrow.title: Can escrow
canEscrow.description: Holders can place balances in escrow.

canTrade.title: Can trade
canTrade.description: Holders can trade on the DEX or AMM.

canTransfer.title: Can transfer
canTransfer.description: Holders can transfer tokens to other accounts.

canClawback.title: Can claw back
canClawback.description: Issuer can reclaim tokens from holders.

metadata.title: Metadata JSON
metadata.placeholder: Paste the generated metadata JSON here

errors.scale: Decimal places must be a whole number from 0 to 255.
errors.maximumPrecision: Maximum supply can have at most {{scale}} decimal places.
errors.maximumRange: Maximum supply must resolve to 1–9,223,372,036,854,775,807 base units.
errors.fee: Transfer fee must be from 0 to 50 with up to 3 decimals.
errors.feeTransfer: Enable Can transfer to set a transfer fee.
errors.metadataSize: Metadata is {{count}} bytes; the limit is 1024.
errors.domain: Domain ID must contain exactly 64 hexadecimal characters.
errors.domainAuth: Enable Require authorization to use a permissioned domain.

mpt-metadata-generator.seo-description: Create XLS-0089 compliant metadata for Multi-Purpose Tokens on the XRP Ledger.
mpt-metadata-generator.intro: Create MPToken metadata according to the current
mpt-metadata-generator.icon-preview-error: The icon could not be rendered.

hints.ticker: Uppercase letters and digits only, up to 6 characters.
hints.icon-uri: The https:// protocol may be omitted; HTTPS is assumed. IPFS and inline data:image URIs are also supported.
hints.uri: The https:// protocol may be omitted; HTTPS is assumed. Complete IPFS and data URIs are also supported.
hints.other-links: Links inside additional information must include the full URI and protocol.

warnings.ticker: Ticker is required and must contain 1–6 uppercase letters or digits.
warnings.name: Token name is required.
warnings.icon-uri: Icon URI must be a valid HTTPS address, IPFS URI, or data:image URI.
warnings.asset-subclass: Asset subclass is required for RWA tokens.
warnings.uri: Each related link needs both a URI and a title.
warnings.uri-format: Each related link must be a valid HTTPS address or a complete URI, including data URIs.
warnings.additional-json: Additional information must be valid JSON.
warnings.additional-object: Additional information JSON must be an object.
warnings.size: Metadata is {{count}} bytes and exceeds the {{max}}-byte on-ledger limit.

asset-class-descriptions.rwa: Real-world assets backed by legally enforceable claims on physical or off-chain financial assets.
asset-class-descriptions.memes: Community and internet-culture tokens without intrinsic backing or utility claims.
asset-class-descriptions.wrapped: Assets from other blockchains, typically backed 1:1 by a bridge or custodian.
asset-class-descriptions.gaming: Tokens used as currency, assets, or rewards in games and virtual worlds.
asset-class-descriptions.defi: Tokens used by DeFi protocols, including governance, DEX, and lending assets.
asset-class-descriptions.other: Experimental or unique tokens that do not fit another class.

asset-subclass-descriptions.stablecoin: Tokens pegged to a stable value and backed by cash, treasuries, or crypto collateral.
asset-subclass-descriptions.commodity: Claims on physical commodities such as gold, silver, or oil.
asset-subclass-descriptions.real_estate: Ownership of or claims on real estate, including fractional property interests.
asset-subclass-descriptions.private_credit: Private debt obligations such as loans, invoices, or receivables.
asset-subclass-descriptions.equity: Ownership shares in a company, similar to traditional stock.
asset-subclass-descriptions.treasury: Tokens backed by or referencing government bills or bonds.
asset-subclass-descriptions.other: Experimental, hybrid, or emerging real-world asset types.

toml-generator.seo-description: Create {{tomlName}} entries for {{ledgerName}} accounts, validators, servers, and tokens.
toml-generator.intro: Create a {{tomlName}} file and place it at
toml-generator.mode-title: What do you want to add?

mode-descriptions.principal: Entity or individual operating the domain, server, validator, and related accounts or tokens.
mode-descriptions.account: Any ledger account you own and want to publicly associate with this domain.
mode-descriptions.token: For token issuers. Adds issuer account, token, and currency metadata.
mode-descriptions.server: Use this if you operate an xrpld node.
mode-descriptions.validator: Use this if you run a validator and want to verify its domain.

notice.before: The file must be served over HTTPS. Use
notice.after: so explorers and wallets can read it.

cors-guide.title: How to enable CORS
cors-guide.intro: Use one of these examples for {{tomlName}}. Adjust the path if your server config already points to the .well-known directory.
cors-guide.managed-text: Look for custom headers, response headers, or CORS rules in your hosting panel and add Access-Control-Allow-Origin with the value * for this TOML file only.

hints.attestation: Generated by validator-keys set_domain. Do not publish the validator token.
hints.account-address-before: Add the account to the TOML file, then
hints.account-address-link: verify it on the domain verification page

warnings.account-address: The account address does not look like a valid classic address.
warnings.issuer-address: The issuer address does not look like a valid classic address.
warnings.rwa-subclass: RWA token metadata should include an asset subclass.
warnings.display-decimals: Display decimals should be a whole number.
warnings.validator-key: Validator public keys usually start with n.
warnings.network-asn: Network ASN should be a whole number.

validator-guide.title: How to verify a validator domain
validator-guide.intro: Validator domain verification needs a two-way link between the domain and the validator.
validator-guide.domain-text: Serve {{tomlName}} from the domain and include a [[VALIDATORS]] entry with the validator public key and attestation.
validator-guide.validator-text: On a machine that is not your validator, use the validator-keys tool with your validator-keys.json file:
validator-guide.after-command: Copy the attestation from the output into the form below.
validator-guide.xrpld-text: Update xrpld.cfg with the generated validator token and restart xrpld.
validator-guide.warning: Do not publish the validator token. Only the attestation belongs in the TOML file.
